description: Conservative default skill allowlist for Windows desktop/Web UI environments. Skills not listed here remain installable, but are hidden from automatic model selection while Windows safe mode is enabled.

skills.name: plan
skills.reason: Planning workflow that writes workspace-relative markdown plans and does not depend on platform-specific shell commands.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.reason: Implementation planning guidance; no operating-system-specific commands.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.reason: Code review workflow guidance that is independent of the host OS.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.reason: TDD workflow guidance that adapts to the repository's own test commands.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.reason: Debugging method guidance that is independent of Unix-only tooling.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.reason: Agent workflow guidance that does not require Linux/macOS commands.

skills.name: hermes-agent
skills.reason: Core Hermes product setup and troubleshooting reference; useful in the Windows desktop app despite some optional Unix examples.

skills.name: native-mcp
skills.reason: Hermes native MCP usage guidance with weak OS coupling.

skills.name: github-auth
skills.reason: GitHub authentication flow based on git/gh, both supported on Windows.

skills.name: github-issues
skills.reason: GitHub issue workflow using API/gh concepts that are available on Windows.

skills.name: github-repo-management
skills.reason: Repository management workflow using git/gh, both supported on Windows.

skills.name: github-pr-workflow
skills.reason: Pull request workflow using git/gh, both supported on Windows.

skills.name: github-code-review
skills.reason: Pull request review workflow; any shell helper is optional and can be replaced by gh/git calls.

skills.name: codebase-inspection
skills.reason: Codebase inspection guidance using Python ecosystem tools.

skills.name: arxiv
skills.reason: Uses arXiv web/API workflows rather than platform-specific local services.

skills.name: duckduckgo-search
skills.reason: Privacy-friendly web search workflow; preinstalled skill guidance uses built-in web search first and treats ddgr CLI as optional.

skills.name: polymarket
skills.reason: Uses public web/API workflows rather than platform-specific commands.

skills.name: dogfood
skills.reason: Web application QA workflow that is browser-oriented and OS-neutral.

skills.name: find-nearby
skills.reason: OpenStreetMap/web lookup workflow with no Unix-only dependency.

skills.name: ideation
skills.reason: Creative ideation workflow with no command-line dependency.

skills.name: excalidraw
skills.reason: Generates Excalidraw JSON for browser-based editing.

skills.name: architecture-diagram
skills.reason: Diagram generation guidance using portable static assets.

skills.name: baoyu-infographic
skills.reason: Infographic design guidance that does not require Unix package managers.

skills.name: popular-web-designs
skills.reason: Web UI design reference material suitable for the Windows Web UI product.

skills.name: songwriting-and-ai-music
skills.reason: Prompting and writing guidance with no platform-specific runtime.

skills.name: linear
skills.reason: Linear API workflow; requires credentials but not Unix-only tooling.

skills.name: notion
skills.reason: Notion API workflow; requires credentials but not Unix-only tooling.

skills.name: youtube-content
skills.reason: Content workflow based on Python/web APIs, suitable for Windows with dependencies installed.

skills.name: gif-search
skills.reason: Tenor/API workflow; optional command-line parsing tools can be replaced on Windows.

skills.name: ocr-and-documents
skills.reason: Document/OCR workflow based mainly on Python packages and optional web extraction.

skills.name: powerpoint
skills.reason: Office document workflow implemented with Python tooling and bundled schemas.

skills.name: llm-wiki
skills.reason: Markdown knowledge-base workflow; Linux service examples are optional.
